title: olearia stricta subsp. stricta
intro: Olearia stricta subsp. stricta, commonly known as the New Zealand tree daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to promote healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Olearia stricta subsp. stricta include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve overall well-being.
